info.title: NP OPEN API

get.description:
This is a method to get information about the current state of the API. If everything is OK, you will receive an "OK" message. Otherwise, you'll see some error.

#### SUCCESSFUL RESPONSE FIELDS

| **Name** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| message | String | Current status of our API. |

Set-Cookie.example: __cfduid=d100179a7e095065cf498b0371b423b7d1605288381; expires=Sun, 13-Dec-20 17:26:21 GMT; path=/; domain=.nowpayments.io; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax

post.description:
Authentication method for obtaining a JWT token. You should specify your email and password which you are using for signing in into dashboard.  
JWT token will be required for using 'Get list of payments' and 'Create payout' endpoints. For security reasons, JWT tokens expire in 5 minutes.

**Please note** that email and password fields in this request are case-sensitive. [test@gmail.com](https://mailto:test@gmail.com) does not equal to [Test@gmail.com](https://mailto:Test@gmail.com)

#### SUCCESSFUL RESPONSE FIELDS

| **Name** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| token | String | Token that is required to execute certain commands via API. |

parameters.name: fixed_rate
parameters.description: (Optional) Returns avaliable currencies with minimum and maximum amount of the exchange.

get.description: This is a method for obtaining information about all cryptocurrencies available for payments for your current setup of payout wallets.

application/json.example:
"currencies": [
 {
 "id": 121,
 "code": "AAVE",
 "name": "Aave",
 "enable": true,
 "wallet_regex": "^(0x)[0-9A-Fa-f]{40}$",
 "priority": 127,
 "extra_id_exists": false,
 "extra_id_regex": null,
 "logo_url": "/images/coins/aave.svg",
 "track": true,
 "cg_id": "aave",
 "is_maxlimit": false,
 "network": "eth",
 "smart_contract": null,
 "network_precision": null
 }
]

get.description: This is a method to obtain detailed information about all cryptocurrencies available for payments.
This is a method for obtaining information about the cryptocurrencies available for payments. Shows the coins you set as available for payments in the "coins settings" tab on your personal account.

parameters.name: fiat_equivalent
parameters.description: (Optional) Mentioning ticker of any supported fiat currency you can get fiat equivalent of calculated minimal amount

parameters.name: is_fixed_rate
parameters.description: (Optional) Set this as true if you're using fixed rate flow

parameters.name: is_fee_paid_by_user
parameters.description: (Optional) Set this as true if you're using fee paid by user flow

get.description:
Get the minimum payment amount for a specific pair.

You can provide both currencies in the pair or just currency_from, and we will calculate the minimum payment amount for currency_from and currency which you have specified as the outcome in the Payment Settings.

You can also specify one of the fiat currencies in the currency_from. In this case, the minimum payment will be calculated in this fiat currency.

You can also add field fiat_equivalent (optional field) to get the fiat equivalent of the minimum amount.

"is_fixed_rate", and "is_fee_paid_by_user" parameters allows you to see current minimum amounts for corresponsing flows (it may differ from the standard flow!)

In the case of several outcome wallets we will calculate the minimum amount in the same way we route your payment to a specific wallet.

#### SUCCESSFUL RESPONSE FIELDS

| **Name** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| currency_from | String | Payin currency |
| currency_to | String | Outcome currency |
| min_amount | Float | Minimal amount for payment using mentioned currencies |
| fiat_equivalent | Float | (Optional) Get the fiat equivalent for calculated minimal amount |

post.description:
This endpoint is required to get the current estimate on the payment and update the current estimate.  
Please note! Calling this estimate before `expiration_estimate_date` will return the current estimate, it won’t be updated.

`:id` - payment ID, for which you want to get the estimate

Response:  
`id` - payment ID  
`token_id` - id of api key used to create this payment (please discard this parameter)  
`pay_amount` - payment estimate, the exact amount the user will have to send to complete the payment  
`expiration_estimate_date` - expiration date of this estimate

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Payment ID, for which you want to get the estimate

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The limit of items listed in one page of response body. Possible values: from 1 to 500

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: If amount of items is more than specified in 'limit' parameter, you can switch pages with this one. Possible values: from 0 to last existing page number digit.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sorting  by a certain parameter value

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: Set the order of listed items. It can be 'asc' or 'desc'

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Parameters for filtering items by  certain dates

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Parameters for filtering items by certain dates

parameters.name: invoiceId
parameters.description: Filtering items by certain invoice ID

get.description:
This is a method for calculating the approximate price in cryptocurrency for a given value in Fiat currency. You will need to provide the initial cost in the Fiat currency (amount, currency_from) and the necessary cryptocurrency (currency_to)  
Currently following fiat currencies are available: USD, EUR, CAD, GBP, AUD, ILS, RON.

Please note that this method allows you to get estimates for crypto pairs as well.

post.description:
This method is required to verify payouts by using your 2FA code.  
You’ll have 10 attempts to verify the payout. If it is not verified after 10 attempts, the payout will remain in ‘creating’ status.  
Payout will be processed only when it is verified.

If you have 2FA app enabled in your dashboard, payouts will accept 2FA code from your app. Otherwise the code for payouts validation will be sent to your registration email.

Please take a note that unverified payouts will be automatically rejected in an hour after creation.

Next is a description of the required request fields:

- :batch-withdrawal-id - payout id you received in `2. Create payout` method;
    
- verification_code - 2fa code you received with your Google Auth app or via email;
    

In order to establish an automatic verification of payouts, you should switch 2FA through the application.  
There are several libraries for different frameworks aimed on generating 2FA codes based on a secret key from your account settings, for example, Speakeasy for JavaScript.  
We do not recommend to change any default settings.

```
const 2faVerificationCode = speakeasy.totp({
      your_2fa_secret_key,
      encoding: 'base32',
})

 ```

parameters.name: batch-withdrawal-id
parameters.description: Batch withdrawal ID of the batch you want to verify

post.description:
This method allows you to **cancel a scheduled/recurring payout** that was previously created with the **`execute_at`** parameter. Only payouts that are scheduled in advance can be canceled using this endpoint. Once canceled, the payout will receive the status **`cancelled`**.

Please note: You must specify the **payout id** of the individual payout you wish to cancel, not the batch id.

**Params:**

- payout_id - the unique identifier of the planned payout you want to cancel. This should be the ID of the **individual payout**, not the batch ID.

parameters.name: payout_id
parameters.description: Id of payout you want to request details for.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: The coin ticker of your payout

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The desired amount of your payout

get.description:
This endpoint will show you the estimated amount of network fee for payout.

Please note that:

- This endpoint will show you the fee estimate for the moment of the request is done. Fee itself is dynamic and may change over a brief moment of time.
    
- The fee depends on the blockchain itself and its congestion. We cannot affect it in any way nor control or hold it.
    
- The actual fee will be calcualated at the moment of payout processing. Please be aware that it can differ from estimate.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to cancel an entire recurring payout batch.

Once canceled:

- No new payouts will be generated within the batch
    
- Existing payouts that have not yet been processed will be canceled
    
- The batch status will be updated accordingly
    

**Params:**

**batch_id** (required) - the unique identifier of the payout batch you want to cancel.

This method is used for crypto payouts only. Please note that payouts can be requested only using a whitelisted IP address, and to whitelisted wallet addresses. It's a security measure enabled for each partner account by default.

You can whitelist both of these anytime in your NOWPayments personal account by visiting 'Whitelist' section in 'Settings'.

You need to provide your data as a JSON-object payload. Next is a description of the required request fields:

- execute_at (required for scheduled/recurring payouts only) – the date and time when the payout(s) should be executed. Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`;
    
- interval (required for scheduled/recurring payouts only) – defines how the payout should be executed:
    

`ONETIME` – payout is executed once;  
`DAILY` – payouts are created every day;  
`WEEKLY` – payouts are created every 7 days;  
`MONTHLY_BY_DAY_OF_MONTH` – payouts are created monthly on the same calendar date;  
`MONTHLY_BY_DAY_OF_WEEK` – payouts are created monthly on the same weekday pattern;

- interval_value (optional; for scheduled/recurring payouts only) – defines how often payouts should be created within the selected interval (default is `1`).  
    Example: `interval = DAILY`, `interval_value = 3` → payout every 3 days;
    
- execute_until (optional; for scheduled/recurring payouts only**)** – the date and time after which new payouts should no longer be created for the batch. Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`;
    
- address (required) - the address where you want to send funds;
    
- currency (required) - payout currency;
    
- amount (required) - amount of the payout. **Must not exceed 6 decimals** (i.e. 0.123456);
    
- extra_id (optional) - memo, destination tag, etc.
    
- ipn_callback_url(optional) - url to receive callbacks, should contain "http" or "https", eg. "[https://nowpayments.io"](https://nowpayments.io). Please note: you can either set ipn_callback_url for each individual payout, or for all payouts in a batch (see example). **In both cases IPNs will be sent for each payout separately;**
    
- payout_description(optional) - a description of the payout. You can set it for all payouts in a batch;
    
- unique_external_id(optional) - a unique external identifier;
    
- fiat_amount(optional) - used for setting the payout amount in fiat equivalent. **Overrides "amount" parameter;**
    
- fiat_currency (optional) - used for determining fiat currency to get the fiat equivalent for. **Required for "fiat_amount" parameter to work. DOES NOT override "currency" parameter. Payouts are made in crypto only, no fiat payouts are available unless you have a fiat off-ramp provider activated for your account;**
    

Here is the list of the available payout statuses:

- creating;
    
- waiting;
    
- processing;
    
- sending;
    
- finished;
    
- failed;
    
- rejected;
    

Please note that in some cases 'Failed' payouts may be processed after your confirmation, so please check this possibility with the support team beforer recreating the payout.

Please, take note that you may request a single payout, but it still should be formatted as an array with the single element.

### How to schedule a payout

Scheduled Payouts allow you to define a specific time when the payout should be executed. This is useful when you need to ensure that funds are sent at a precise moment.  
Please ensure that your IP address and wallet addresses are whitelisted. Payouts can only be requested from whitelisted IPs and sent to whitelisted addresses by default.

**Step 1: Define the Parameters for Scheduled Payout**

To create a scheduled payout, you need to provide two key parameters before the regular payout details:

- **execute_at** (required):  
    Specify the exact date and time when the payout should be processed. Use the format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`.  
    Example: `"2026-02-29T10:35:00.000Z"`
    
- **interval** (required):  
    Set the frequency for the payout. **Currently, only** **`ONETIME`** **is supported, meaning the payout will occur once at the specified time.**
    

**Step 2: Add Payout Details**

Next, provide the details of the payout, such as the amount, currency, and address where the funds should be sent. You can find all the required fields in the description above.

**Step 3: Create the Payout**

Once you have defined the `execute_at` and `interval` parameters, and added the payout details, send the request. Make sure to verify it with 2fa using **POST Verify payout endpoint.**

#### **How to cancel a scheduled payout**

If you need to cancel a scheduled payout, you can use the `POST /v1/payout/w_id/cancel` endpoint. You need to provide the ID of the payout in the request.  
The payout will receive the status `cancelled` after being successfully canceled.

### How to create recurring payout(s)

1. specify the exact date and time **when the first payout(s) should be executed**, in the format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. with the **`execute_at`** param (required for this flow). For example: `"2026-02-29T10:35:00.000Z"`
    
2. specify the desired `interval` (required) which defines how often payouts should be created:
    

- **`DAILY`** — payouts are created every day
    
- **`WEEKLY`** — payouts are created every 7 days
    
- **`MONTHLY_BY_DAY_OF_MONTH`** — payouts are created monthly on the same date
    
- **`MONTHLY_BY_DAY_OF_WEEK`** — payouts are created monthly on the same weekday pattern (e.g., second Monday)
    

3\. specify an `interval_value` (optional) which defines how frequently the interval repeats:

**Examples:**

- `DAILY` + `interval_value = 3` → every 3 days
    
- `WEEKLY` + `interval_value = 2` → every 2 weeks
    

Default value: `1`

Maximum value: 365 for `DAILY` / 52 for `WEEKLY` / 12 for `MONTHLY`

4\. specify `execute_until` param (optional) if you want the system to stop creating new payouts after a specific date and time. For example: `"2026-05-29T10:35:00.000Z"`

After this date/time, no new payouts will be generated.

5\. specify other payout details like amount, currency, and address in the request payload and send the request using **POST /v1/payout** method.

#### How recurring payouts work:

- A **batch** (which contains one or more payouts) is created automatically when using recurring intervals
    
- The first payout date is scheduled using `execute_at`
    
- Future payouts are generated automatically based on:
    
    - `interval`
        
    - `interval_value`
        
- Payouts continue to be created until:
    
    - `execute_until` is reached, or
        
    - the batch is canceled
        

#### Canceling recurring payouts

1. Cancel a single payout: **POST /v1/payout/:payout_id/cancel**
    

- Cancels **only the selected payout**
    
- Does **not** stop other payouts in the batch
    

2\. Cancel an entire batch: **POST /v1/payout/:batch_id/cancel-batch**

- Cancels all the **payouts this particular batch contains**
    
- Stops all the payouts in the batch from future creation

get.description:
This endpoint shows you current minimal amount for withdrawals.

Parameters:

:coin - ticker of the coin.
